children.heading: Grants
children.label: a Grants
children.text: The Secretary, acting through the Assistant Secretary, shall award grants to eligible entities to enable such entities to provide for improved emergency medical services in rural areas or to residents of rural areas.

children.label: A
children.text: an emergency medical services agency operated by a local or tribal government (including fire-based and non-fire based); or

children.label: B
children.text: an emergency medical services agency that is described in and exempt from tax under ; andsection 501(c) of title 26section 501(a) of title 26
submit an application to the Secretary at such time, in such manner, and containing such information as the Secretary may require.

children.label: A
children.text: train emergency medical services personnel as appropriate to obtain and maintain licenses and certifications relevant to service in an emergency medical services agency described in subsection (b)(1);

children.label: B
children.text: conduct courses that qualify graduates to serve in an emergency medical services agency described in subsection (b)(1) in accordance with State and local requirements;

children.label: C
children.text: fund specific training to meet Federal or State licensing or certification requirements;

children.label: D
children.text: acquire emergency medical services equipment; and

children.label: E
children.text: ensure emergency medical services personnel are trained on mental health and substance use disorders and care for individuals with such disorders in emergency situations; and

children.label: A
children.text: recruit and retain emergency medical services personnel, which may include volunteer personnel;

children.label: B
children.text: develop new ways to educate emergency health care providers through the use of technology-enhanced educational methods;

children.label: C
children.text: acquire personal protective equipment for emergency medical services personnel as required by the Occupational Safety and Health Administration; or

children.label: D
children.text: acquire drugs or devices approved, cleared, or otherwise legally marketed under the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act [ et seq.] for emergency treatment of known or suspected overdose.21 U.S.C. 301

children.heading: Grant amounts
children.label: d Grant amounts
children.text: Each grant awarded under this section shall be in an amount not to exceed $200,000.

children.label: A
children.text: means resources used by a public or private nonprofit licensed entity to deliver medical care outside of a medical facility under emergency conditions that occur as a result of the condition of the patient; and

children.label: B
children.text: includes services delivered (either on a compensated or volunteer basis) by an emergency medical services provider or other provider that is licensed or certified by the State involved as an emergency medical technician, a paramedic, or an equivalent professional (as determined by the State).

children.label: A
children.text: a nonmetropolitan statistical area;

children.label: B
children.text: an area designated as a rural area by any law or regulation of a State; or

children.label: C
children.text: a rural census tract of a metropolitan statistical area (as determined under the most recent rural urban commuting area code as set forth by the Office of Management and Budget).

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to carry out this section such sums as may be necessary for each of fiscal years 2024 through 2028.

children.heading: Administrative costs
children.label: 2 Administrative costs
children.text: The Secretary may use not more than 10 percent of the amount appropriated under paragraph (1) for a fiscal year for the administrative expenses of carrying out this section.
